extraction.one_line_summary: BERNAL Egan won GC by 1:29 over CARUSO Damiano after defending pink in Milan ITT won by GANNA Filippo (5th consecutive Giro ITT victory).
tactical_patterns: GANNA Filippo absorbed 30-second bike change 1.5km from finish yet still won by 12 seconds, revealing massive ITT dominance buffer.
ALMEIDA João posted top-5 ITT result on final stage, showing young riders can maintain power freshness after three-week GC battle.
CARUSO Damiano gained time on BERNAL in final ITT but 1:29 deficit proved insurmountable; older riders finish strong physically.
CAVAGNA Rémi mechanical 500m from finish cost potential stage win, showing Ghana's margin wasn't as secure as results suggest.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: Five consecutive Giro ITT wins establishes him as uniquely dominant on Italian Grand Tour chrono parcours regardless of length.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Won Giro on first attempt while managing 1:29 GC defense in final ITT against specialist opposition.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: First Grand Tour podium at age 33+ by gaining time on GC winner in final ITT shows late-career peak.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Top-5 ITT finish after three weeks signals exceptional recovery capacity for young stage racer.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Maintained third place without gaining time on CARUSO, suggesting ITT remains relative weakness even when fresh.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: negative: Mechanical 500m from finish while challenging for stage win indicates bike handling risk in technical urban ITTs.
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: positive: Second place ITT result 12 seconds behind Ghana establishes him as emerging Italian TT talent.
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: positive: Moved up in final GC standings via strong ITT performance, showing climbing domestiques can gain late positions.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Won points jersey (first Giro ciclamino) despite not winning final ITT stage, confirming consistency over peak power.
